purpose: Classified parity divergences between mixeff (Rust default optimizer cobyla / pattern_search) and lme4-family references (BOBYQA / NEWUOA via nlopt). Each entry locks one (case_id, field) pair to a documented status with regression-detector bounds (observed_max_abs_diff, observed_max_rel_diff). PRD §3 disclaims bit-exact lme4 reproduction; PRD §11 cites this file as the authoritative ledger.

status_values.expected_mismatch: Documented numeric drift attributable to optimizer/path differences. The recorded bounds are an upper limit; tests fail if observed diffs exceed them.
status_values.upstream_bug: Rust-engine bug uncovered by parity. Same regression-detector contract as expected_mismatch, plus a linked upstream mote tracking the fix.
status_values.unsupported: Field is not currently certified by the Rust contract for this case. The wrapper records the observed diff but does not assert.
status_values.design_weak_identifiability: Field is not asserted because the design makes this component weakly identifiable or aliased with fixed-effect structure; user-facing diagnostics explain the limitation.
status_values.pass: (Implicit) No ledger entry. The strict case tolerance applies; any failure is unclassified and must be triaged before merge.

policy.tightening_rule: If observed_max_abs_diff (re-measured during scoreboard runs) drops below 25% of the recorded bound, tighten the bound or remove the entry.
policy.regression_rule: Tests fail when observed_max_abs_diff or observed_max_rel_diff exceeds the recorded bound. Either bound may be omitted; at least one of expected_max_abs_diff or expected_max_rel_diff must be present.
policy.rel_bound_omission: Omit expected_max_rel_diff for fields whose values cross zero (e.g. residuals), since relative-diff is degenerate near zero. Use only the absolute bound.

mismatches.formula: trans_abs_stom ~ il + (1 | row) + (1 | col)
mismatches.reason: Optimizer drift: Rust default cobyla vs lme4 BOBYQA on the 727-row 3-grouping crossed-intercepts drop_tray model. logLik agrees to ~2e-8; per-observation residuals drift by up to ~8e-5. Relative-diff bound omitted because residuals cross zero (degenerate ratio). PRD §3 disclaims bit-exactness; PRD risk R2 anticipates this class of divergence.

mismatches.formula: trans_abs_stom ~ il + (1 | row) + (1 | col)
mismatches.reason: Optimizer drift: column-grouping intercept SD differs by ~7.1e-5 (~1.4e-3 relative) between mixeff cobyla optimum and lme4 BOBYQA optimum. Variance components are notoriously sensitive to optimizer choice on small-magnitude grouping factors; PRD risk R2 covers this.

mismatches.formula: everest_feet ~ anchor + (1 + anchor | referrer)
mismatches.reason: Optimizer drift in a near-boundary correlated random-slope model (|corr| ≈ 0.88). After the 'anchor_treatment' basis transformation in sdamr_expected_varcorr(), the marginal SD for the high-anchor intercept drifts by ~18.5 SD-units (~9e-3 relative). The reduced-rank/identifiability surface near the random-slope boundary makes per-component SDs sensitive to optimizer choice; PRD risk R2 covers this.

mismatches.formula: everest_feet ~ anchor + (1 + anchor | referrer)
mismatches.reason: Optimizer drift in the same near-boundary correlated random-slope model (|corr| ≈ 0.88). The marginal SD for the low-anchor slope drifts by ~41 SD-units (~7e-3 relative). PRD risk R2 covers this.

mismatches.formula: everest_feet ~ anchor_contrast + (1 | referrer) + (0 + anchor_contrast | referrer)
mismatches.reason: Optimizer drift on the uncorrelated split-block random-slope variant. mixeff and lme4 agree on logLik to ~4e-4 and on residual SD to ~3e-5 (relative), but the slope SD for anchor_contrast differs by ~24 SD-units (~4e-3 relative). Same near-boundary identifiability issue as the correlated variant; PRD risk R2 covers this.

mismatches.formula: strength ~ 1 + (1 | batch) + (1 | cask)
mismatches.reason: Optimizer drift between mixeff cobyla and lme4 BOBYQA on the two-level nested-style crossed-intercepts Pastes model. logLik / sigma / fixef agree to ~1e-6 but conditional modes on the inner cask grouping differ by up to ~5.5e-5 (~2.5e-4 relative). Variance components on near-zero residual blocks are sensitive to optimizer path; PRD §3 disclaims bit-exact lme4 reproduction and PRD risk R2 covers this class of divergence. Reference: lme4 2.0.1, lmerTest 3.2.1, pbkrtest 0.5.5.

mismatches.package_source: Bodo Winter LME tutorial politeness example
mismatches.formula: frequency ~ attitude + gender + (1 + attitude | subject) + (1 + attitude | scenario)
mismatches.reason: Optimizer drift in the Bodo Winter correlated random-slope politeness model. mixeff and lme4 agree on sigma within ~1e-3 and fixed effects within case tolerance, but the scenario attitude slope SD differs by ~0.086 (~1.54e-2 relative). Variance components in small correlated random-slope models are sensitive to optimizer path; PRD §3 and risk R2 cover this class of non-bit-exact parity.

mismatches.package_source: Bodo Winter LME tutorial politeness example
mismatches.formula: frequency ~ attitude + gender + (1 + attitude | subject) + (1 + attitude | scenario)
mismatches.reason: Same Bodo Winter correlated random-slope model as the scenario slope entry. The subject attitude slope SD differs by ~0.0133 (~1.01e-2 relative), just outside the strict tutorial tolerance while core likelihood-scale quantities remain close. Keep this classified as bounded optimizer drift.

mismatches.package_source: Bodo Winter LME tutorial politeness example
mismatches.formula: frequency ~ gender + (1 + attitude | subject) + (1 + attitude | scenario)
mismatches.reason: Residual SD in the Bodo Winter random-slope null-gender model differs by ~0.0272 (~1.08e-3 relative), marginally above the strict tutorial tolerance. The model is a small correlated random-slope fit where optimizer path differences move variance components slightly; track as bounded expected mismatch.

mismatches.package_source: Bodo Winter LME tutorial politeness example
mismatches.formula: frequency ~ gender + (1 + attitude | subject) + (1 + attitude | scenario) vs frequency ~ attitude + gender + (1 + attitude | subject) + (1 + attitude | scenario)
mismatches.reason: The Bodo Winter random-slope likelihood-ratio p-value differs by ~3.5e-6 absolute (~3.6e-4 relative) because the nested fits land at slightly different optimizer optima. The LRT statistic itself remains within the strict 1e-3 tolerance; classify the p-value drift as bounded expected mismatch.

mismatches.package_source: lme4 cbpp dataset expanded to Bernoulli rows
mismatches.formula: y ~ period + (1 | herd)
mismatches.reason: The initial R GLMM bridge exposes the upstream profiled fast-PIRLS path, whereas lme4::glmer uses a joint Laplace optimizer. Fixed-effect estimates are close in sign and scale but not strict-lme4-parity quantities under this approximation.

mismatches.package_source: lme4 cbpp dataset expanded to Bernoulli rows
mismatches.formula: y ~ period + (1 | herd)
mismatches.reason: Same profiled fast-PIRLS versus joint-Laplace distinction as the cbpp fixed-effect entry; covariance scale is bounded but not strict lme4 parity.

mismatches.package_source: lme4 cbpp dataset expanded to Bernoulli rows
mismatches.formula: y ~ period + (1 | herd)
mismatches.reason: Normalized log-likelihood is close but differs slightly because profiled fast-PIRLS and lme4 joint Laplace optimize different GLMM approximation paths.

mismatches.package_source: lme4 cbpp dataset expanded to Bernoulli rows
mismatches.formula: y ~ period + (1 | herd)
mismatches.reason: Deviance parity is measured as -2*logLik on both sides. The bounded difference follows the same profiled fast-PIRLS versus joint-Laplace approximation distinction.

mismatches.formula: TICKS ~ YEAR + cHEIGHT + (1 | INDEX) + (1 | BROOD) + (1 | LOCATION)
mismatches.reason: The grouseticks multi-random-intercept Poisson GLMM is a larger profiled fast-PIRLS versus lme4 joint-Laplace comparison. Fixed-effect estimates are bounded but not promoted as strict lme4 parity under the current method.

mismatches.formula: TICKS ~ YEAR + cHEIGHT + (1 | INDEX) + (1 | BROOD) + (1 | LOCATION)
mismatches.reason: Variance-component scales differ within a bounded range under profiled fast-PIRLS versus lme4 joint Laplace.

mismatches.formula: TICKS ~ YEAR + cHEIGHT + (1 | INDEX) + (1 | BROOD) + (1 | LOCATION)
mismatches.reason: Normalized log-likelihood differs modestly under the current profiled fast-PIRLS path; this is bounded until the joint backend is exposed.

mismatches.formula: TICKS ~ YEAR + cHEIGHT + (1 | INDEX) + (1 | BROOD) + (1 | LOCATION)
mismatches.reason: Deviance parity is measured as -2*logLik on both sides because lme4::deviance.glmer reports the GLM deviance-residual scale. The bounded difference follows the current profiled fast-PIRLS approximation.

mismatches.formula: correct ~ group * mask * soa_s + block + (1 + mask + soa_s || participant) + (1 | item)
mismatches.reason: Real-data intact GLMM uses the upstream profiled fast-PIRLS path, while the cached reference is lme4 joint Laplace. The largest drift is the intercept (~0.466); coefficient-relative bounds are omitted because one interaction coefficient is near zero and crosses sign.

mismatches.formula: correct ~ group * mask * soa_s + block + (1 + mask + soa_s || participant) + (1 | item)
mismatches.reason: Normalized log-likelihood differs because profiled fast-PIRLS and lme4 joint Laplace optimize different GLMM approximation paths for the intact real-data subset.

mismatches.formula: correct ~ group * mask * soa_s + block + (1 + mask + soa_s || participant) + (1 | item)
mismatches.reason: AIC inherits the bounded intact-subset fast-PIRLS versus lme4 joint-Laplace log-likelihood gap.

mismatches.formula: correct ~ group * mask * soa_s * stimtype + block + (1 + mask + soa_s || participant) + (1 | item)
mismatches.reason: Combined real-data GLMM fixed effects have a bounded profiled fast-PIRLS versus lme4 joint-Laplace beta gap, dominated by the intercept and stimtype main effect. Relative bounds are omitted because one interaction coefficient is near zero.

mismatches.package_source: OSF h6rfv Study 2 dataset and lmer script
mismatches.formula: cute ~ con_f * time_f * level_f + (1 | stim) + (1 | sub) + (1 | sub:con_f) + (1 | sub:time_f)
mismatches.reason: The Study 2 raw design has one stimulus for each attractiveness/personality cell, so the stimulus intercept SD is weakly identified against the full fixed factorial cell means. Fixed effects, likelihood-scale summaries, fitted values, and the subject-side variance components remain the functional parity targets.

mismatches.package_source: OSF h6rfv Study 2 dataset and lmer script
mismatches.formula: warmth ~ con_f * time_f * level_f + (1 | stim) + (1 | sub) + (1 | sub:con_f) + (1 | sub:time_f)
mismatches.reason: The Study 2 raw design has one stimulus for each attractiveness/personality cell, so the stimulus intercept SD is weakly identified against the full fixed factorial cell means. Fixed effects, likelihood-scale summaries, fitted values, and the subject-side variance components remain the functional parity targets.

mismatches.package_source: OSF h6rfv Study 2 dataset and lmer script
mismatches.formula: competence ~ con_f * time_f * level_f + (1 | stim) + (1 | sub) + (1 | sub:con_f) + (1 | sub:time_f)
mismatches.reason: The Study 2 raw design has one stimulus for each attractiveness/personality cell, so the stimulus intercept SD is weakly identified against the full fixed factorial cell means. Fixed effects, likelihood-scale summaries, fitted values, and the subject-side variance components remain the functional parity targets.

mismatches.formula: Error ~ OpenPractice * Year + (1 | Source)
mismatches.reason: joint_laplace on the raw (uncentered, large-offset) Year design reports fit_status=converged_interior with a non-finite objective and a sub-optimal interaction coefficient (0.79586 vs the offset-invariant MLE 0.85288 centered / 0.85439 glmer). The interaction is invariant to centering Year, so raw != centered proves the raw fit is off the optimum. lme4 fails loud on the same design (scaling warning; PIRLS step-halving error on harder cases). Estimation parity holds once Year is centered; the offset-invariance assertion in the test is skip()ped pending the upstream fix.

mismatches.formula: Error ~ OpenPractice + (1 | Source)
mismatches.reason: GLMM Wald standard errors are now certified for this joint_laplace fit by the Rust active-Hessian fixed-effect inference table (method=asymptotic_wald_z, status=available) and match lme4::glmer within the strict parity tolerance; retained as a resolved ledger row for the former unsupported gap.
